questions.explanation: The Android SDK includes platform APIs, build tools, emulator, and debugging tools needed to develop Android applications.

options.A: Providing tools, APIs, and libraries to build, test, and debug Android apps
options.B: Hosting your app backend and database automatically
options.D: Replacing the Android operating system on a device

questions.question: In Android development, what is the Android SDK primarily used for?

questions.explanation: Google recommends Kotlin as the primary language for Android development due to its safety features and interoperability with Java.
questions.question: Which language is the officially recommended primary language for modern Android app development?

questions.explanation: Gradle configures builds, manages dependencies, and defines build variants/flavors for Android apps.

options.C: Encrypting local database files by default

questions.question: What is Gradle most commonly used for in Android projects?

questions.explanation: An if statement evaluates a condition and executes different code paths depending on whether it is true or false.

options.A: Conditional branching based on a boolean expression
options.B: A loop that always runs a fixed number of times
options.C: A way to declare a class

questions.question: In programming, what does an "if" statement primarily implement?

questions.explanation: Retrofit uses method annotations like @GET, @POST, @PUT, and @DELETE to define HTTP operations.
questions.question: In Retrofit, which annotation is typically used to perform an HTTP GET request?

questions.explanation: launch starts a new coroutine without blocking the current thread and is commonly used with lifecycle-aware scopes like viewModelScope.
questions.question: In Kotlin coroutines, which builder is commonly used to start a coroutine tied to a lifecycle-aware scope (e.g., in ViewModel) without blocking the thread?

questions.explanation: A DAO declares the database operations; Room generates the implementation to execute SQL safely and efficiently.

options.A: Define methods for database operations (queries/inserts/updates) and map them to SQL
options.B: Render database tables as UI components
options.C: Automatically sync local data to Firebase without code
options.D: Replace the need for entities by storing raw JSON

questions.question: In Room Database, what is the primary purpose of a DAO (Data Access Object)?

questions.explanation: Room can emit updates via Flow; refreshing in a background coroutine and writing to Room updates the Flow, enabling immediate cached display and automatic UI updates.

options.A: Expose a Flow from Room, collect it in the UI, and in parallel refresh via Retrofit in a coroutine that writes results back to Room
options.B: Call Retrofit on the main thread first, then write to Room only if the UI is still visible
options.C: Store API responses only in memory to avoid disk I/O, and never persist to Room
options.D: Disable caching and always fetch from the network to ensure consistency

questions.question: You need offline-first behavior: show cached data immediately from Room, then refresh from a Retrofit API and update the UI. Which approach best fits a modern Android architecture with coroutines?

questions.explanation: remember caches values across recompositions (optionally keyed), preventing expensive work from re-running unless inputs change.

options.A: Wrap the calculation result in remember (or remember(key)) so it is cached across recompositions
options.B: Move the calculation into onCreate() of an Activity
options.C: Use findViewById to cache the result
options.D: Disable recomposition for the entire screen

questions.question: In Jetpack Compose, a list item recomposes frequently and triggers an expensive calculation each time. What is the best Compose-specific fix to avoid repeating the calculation unnecessarily?

questions.explanation: React Native uses JavaScript to drive apps that render native UI components through a framework bridge/runtime.
questions.question: A team is building a cross-platform mobile app using JavaScript and wants a framework that uses native UI components via a bridge. Which mobile device software framework best matches this description?
